reasoning: Human bocavirus (HBoV) does not currently have an approved vaccine, nor are there any widely known vaccine candidates in late-stage clinical trials. Because vaccine development, regulatory approval, and the subsequent updating of reputable sources like Wikipedia take years, it is virtually impossible for a vaccine to be developed and the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' to be updated to 'Yes' within the brief 1-to-3-month timeframe leading up to January 2026. Uncertainty slightly increases for dates further out to account for the minute possibility of un-reverted vandalism on Wikipedia or an obscure experimental candidate erroneously being cataloged as a definitive 'Yes'.
